---
title: "Projects"
subtitle: "The work I spend the most time on. Each links to a longer write-up."
toc: false
---

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card}
[West Nile virus thesis — California mosquito surveillance](projects/wnv-thesis.qmd){.card-title}

A quantitative MPH thesis modeling WNV infection rate in *Culex* mosquito pools
against environmental covariates and surveillance effort, with spatial and
temporal structure handled explicitly on a county-month grid. In progress; no
results claimed yet.

[Spatial epidemiology]{.tag} [Bayesian modeling]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[MESA — Mapping Epidemiology in Sparse Areas](projects/mesa.qmd){.card-title}

A C++ desktop application for public-health mapping where data is thin:
geocoding, Getis-Ord Gi\* and Moran's I, and Monte Carlo permutation testing,
with the statistics validated against PySAL and R.

[Spatial statistics]{.tag} [C++]{.tag} [Desktop software]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[FEMA Hazus — GIS and risk-modeling software](projects/hazus.qmd){.card-title}

Software in support of FEMA's Hazus natural-hazard loss-estimation program:
GIS-driven hazard and risk workflows, spatial data engineering, database
updates, and validation. Kept high-level and sanitized.

[GIS]{.tag} [Risk modeling]{.tag} [C#/C++]{.tag} [SQL Server]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[SEUHD practicum — applied public-health GIS](projects/seuhd-practicum.qmd){.card-title}

Practicum with a local health department: respiratory-illness spatial analysis
with space-time cubes and emerging hot-spot analysis, plus immunization-
compliance reporting from EHR data and geocoded addresses.

[Applied epidemiology]{.tag} [ArcGIS]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

:::

## Code & open source

Smaller public repositories relevant to this work — disease modeling, spatial
methods, and environmental data. The full list, including GIS and other tooling,
is at [github.com/minorsecond](https://github.com/minorsecond).

::: {.repo-list}

::: {.repo-item}
[host-vector-human-model](https://github.com/minorsecond/host-vector-human-model){.repo-name}

A spatial simulation of mosquito-borne transmission linking vector, host, and
human populations over census-tract geography, backed by PostGIS.

[Vector-borne disease]{.tag} [Spatial simulation]{.tag} [PostGIS]{.tag} [Python]{.tag}
:::

::: {.repo-item}
[Epi](https://github.com/minorsecond/Epi){.repo-name}

A configurable SEIR compartmental model for infectious-disease dynamics, with
user-set parameters, stochastic outcomes, and an option for waning immunity.

[Infectious disease]{.tag} [SEIR]{.tag} [Simulation]{.tag} [Perl]{.tag}
:::

::: {.repo-item}
[Chile — Chagas disease risk](https://github.com/minorsecond/Chile){.repo-name}

An early spatial-autocorrelation analysis of Chagas disease risk in Chile —
Moran's I and spatial weights in R.

[Vector-borne disease]{.tag} [Spatial autocorrelation]{.tag} [R]{.tag}
:::

::: {.repo-item}
[bios5300](https://github.com/minorsecond/bios5300){.repo-name}

A graduate biostatistics project analyzing BRFSS survey data: a reproducible
R pipeline examining associations between behavioral and socioeconomic factors
and health outcomes, with logistic regression and a prevalence map.

[Biostatistics]{.tag} [Logistic regression]{.tag} [Survey data]{.tag} [R]{.tag}
:::

::: {.repo-item}
[Chlorophyl](https://github.com/minorsecond/Chlorophyl){.repo-name}

A desktop tool that derives vegetation layers from LiDAR point clouds — the kind
of environmental-covariate extraction that feeds spatial models.

[Remote sensing]{.tag} [Environmental data]{.tag} [Python]{.tag}
:::

::: {.repo-item}
[co_forestfrag_map](https://github.com/minorsecond/co_forestfrag_map){.repo-name}

An interactive web map of forest fragmentation in Colorado.

[Web GIS]{.tag} [Environmental data]{.tag} [JavaScript]{.tag}
:::

:::
